topic: Windsurf Pricing 2026: Plans, Credits and Real Costs Explained

key_points: Official pricing page listing Free, Pro ($20/mo), Max ($200/mo), Teams ($40/user/mo), and Enterprise (custom) tiers
Tab autocomplete is unlimited on all plans including Free
Cascade AI agent usage is quota-controlled with daily and weekly refresh windows
SWE-1 and SWE-1.5 proprietary models available on Pro and above
Quota system replaced credits on March 19, 2026
Daily and weekly quotas reset automatically — spread over time, not front-loadable
Free models do not consume quota; only premium model Cascade and Chat interactions count
Extra usage beyond quota is billed at API list prices per token
SWE-1 model messages are priced per-message, making cost more predictable than token-based frontier models
Windsurf Pro ($20/mo) and Cursor Pro ($20/mo) are now identically priced after March 2026 change
Teams tier: both Windsurf and Cursor charge $40/user/month
Windsurf uses quota-based daily/weekly limits; Cursor uses a monthly credit pool
Windsurf Enterprise includes SSO, RBAC, SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, and FedRAMP High as standard
Cursor's 'Auto' mode requests are unlimited and don't draw from credits; Windsurf's Tab is unlimited but Cascade is quota-gated
Free plan: unlimited Tab, approximately 25 Flow Actions/month, community-only support
Pro plan ($20/mo): all premium models including GPT-5, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro, SWE-1
Max plan ($200/mo): highest quota ceiling for power users needing maximum AI capacity
Teams adds centralized billing, admin analytics, and priority support over Pro
Enterprise includes hybrid deployment, zero data retention default, government compliance options
Before March 19, 2026: Pro was $15/month with 500 credits in a monthly pool
After March 19, 2026: Pro raised to $20/month with quota-based daily/weekly limits
Legacy subscribers (pre-March 2026) are grandfathered at the $15/month rate
The quota shift removed the flexibility to front-load AI usage on intensive sprint days
Add-on credits at $10 for 250 credits ($0.04 per credit) available when quota is exhausted

stats.fact: Windsurf Pro increased from $15/month to $20/month on March 19, 2026 when the pricing model shifted from credits to quotas
Windsurf Free plan includes approximately 25 Cascade Flow Actions per month and unlimited Tab autocomplete
Windsurf Max tier costs $200/month, targeting heavy power users who need the highest available daily and weekly quota ceiling
Teams plan costs $40/user/month — identical pricing to Cursor Teams as of Q2 2026
Enterprise plan starts around $60/user/month (custom) and supports up to 200 users; includes FedRAMP High compliance via Palantir FedStart on AWS GovCloud
A developer doing 15–20 AI Cascade interactions per day typically stays within the Pro quota allocation for most months
Add-on usage beyond quota is charged at API list prices per token; add-on packs available at $10 for 250 credits
Users who subscribed to Pro before March 19, 2026 are grandfathered at the original $15/month rate (price lock)
Windsurf Enterprise is SOC 2 Type II and FedRAMP High accredited, supports HIPAA BAAs, and defaults to zero-data retention on all paid seats
SWE-1 (flagship) and SWE-1.5 (Fast Agent) are Windsurf's proprietary models priced per-message rather than per-token, providing more predictable quota consumption

angles: The March 2026 credit-to-quota switch: hidden cost or user-friendly redesign? Explain why Windsurf made the change and who wins/loses
Free plan reality check: what can you actually build before hitting the 25 Flow Action ceiling?
Grandfathered users at $15 vs new users at $20 — and why this matters if you're evaluating now
Windsurf vs Cursor head-to-head pricing: same sticker price ($20/mo Pro, $40/mo Teams) but fundamentally different billing mechanics
When to upgrade: Free → Pro → Max — clear triggers based on daily usage patterns
Enterprise compliance story: FedRAMP High, HIPAA, SOC 2 — Windsurf's pitch to regulated industries
Real cost modeling: what a solo dev, a 5-person startup team, and a 50-person engineering org actually pay monthly
SWE-1 model pricing advantage: per-message instead of per-token means more predictable bills
